data.title: Hot Chili's latest quarterly report shows significant expansion of La Verde's high-grade core, with drillhole DKD032 recording 529m grading 0.41% Cu and 0.21% Au.
data.description: In Perth, Australia on January 30, 2026, La Verde Diamond Drilling revealed significant expansion in their gold-rich major copper discovery. Results from drillhole DKD032 showed a length of 529 meters with 0.41% copper and 0.21% gold. This confirmed the substantial growth of La Verde's high-grade core.

data.title: Gold drops 6%; Dow falls 400 points. Apple beats estimates with $143.76B revenue for Q1 FY26; shares rise 25%.
data.description: U.S. stocks saw a downturn in trading, with the Dow Jones index falling over 400 points to 48,646.87, a 0.87% drop, while the NASDAQ fell 0.88% to 23,476.27 and the S&P 500 dropped 0.65% to 6,923.38 on Friday. Consumer staples shares gained 0.3%, but materials stocks fell by 1.7%. Apple Inc. reported stronger-than-expected financial results for Q1 fiscal 2026, with revenue of $143.76 billion and earnings of $2.84 per share, surpassing analyst estimates. Robert Half Inc shares surged 25% to $33.81 after reporting better-than-expected quarterly results. Full story available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Bread Financial beats Q4 estimates with $2.07 EPS, $975M in sales; shares at $72.57, Analysts increase forecasts.
data.description: Bread Financial Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: BFH) reported impressive fourth-quarter earnings, surpassing market expectations. The company recorded adjusted earnings of $2.07 per share, exceeding projections. Quarterly sales totaled $975 million, outperforming the estimated $955.819 million. Following this announcement, Bread Financial shares experienced a minor decline of 0.6%, trading at $72.57 on Friday. Analysts adjusted their price targets in response to these results. For more details, visit a financial news website.

data.title: SoFi stock drops after earnings surge. Q4 earnings at 13 cents/share (above 12 cents estimate) & revenue up to $273.72M.2026 guidance predicts 30% member growth & $4.655B in revenue. Adjusted EBITDA at $1.6B.
data.description: Shares of SoFi Technologies Inc (NASDAQ: SOFI) are experiencing a decline on Friday, retracing early gains made on the heels of fourth-quarter results that surpassed expectations. Earnings per share came in at 13 cents, slightly exceeding the 12 cents consensus estimate. Additionally, revenue saw a significant uptick, increasing by $273.72 million compared to the same quarter last year. Looking forward to full-year 2026, SoFi anticipates a growth of total members by a minimum of 30% year-over-year and anticipates generating approximately $4.655 billion in adjusted net revenue, reflecting around 30% growth. The management is projecting adjusted EBITDA of approximately $1.6 billion, suggesting a margin of about 34%. Adjusted net income is forecasted to hit around $825 million, equating to an 18% margin.

data.title: TraXtion names Brad Kokesh as new CEO, replacing Roger Tracy, in Irving, Texas. TraXtion is a provider of automated tire/vehicle inspection solutions.
data.description: TraXtion, based in Irving, Texas, a prominent provider of automated tire and vehicle inspection solutions, has appointed Brad Kokesh as the new President and Chief Executive Officer. Kokesh, formerly the President and Chief Operating Officer, will assume the role following the retirement of Roger Tracy. This transition signifies a new phase for TraXtion as they strive to enhance their presence in the market and drive innovation in products like TreadSpecX, WheelSpec, Dam.

data.title: ISG launches study on insurance service providers, aiming to analyze ecosystem aiding firms, addressing complex needs.
data.description: A research study on service providers aiding insurance firms in managing operations and meeting complex requirements has been initiated by ISG. The study focuses on the evolving ecosystem of providers in this sector.

data.title: Kevin Warsh, Trump's pick for Fed Chair, may struggle to cut borrowing costs due to opposition.
data.description: Kevin M. Warsh, selected by Mr. Trump for the next Federal Reserve chair, may encounter strong opposition if he aims to push for significantly reduced borrowing rates.

data.title: Parker-Hannifin (NYSE: PH) beats Q2 estimates, posts $7.65 EPS vs $7.17 est. Sales reach $5.174B exceeding $5.066B est. FY26 EPS guidance raised to $26.26-$26.86. CEO acknowledges strong performance.
data.description: Parker-Hannifin Corporation (PH) released its second-quarter financial results, surpassing expectations and increasing its FY26 guidance. The company reported an adjusted EPS of $7.65, exceeding the estimated $7.17. Net sales stood at $5.174 billion, higher than the expected $5.066 billion, marking a 9% year-over-year increase with organic sales growth at 6.6%. CEO Jenny Parmentier credited the stellar quarter to the global team's efforts, the effectiveness of The Win Strategy business model, and the robustness of the current portfolio. The company updated its GAAP EPS guidance for FY26 to $26.26 to $26.86, up from $25.53 to $26.33, against a $26.46 estimate. Furthermore, the adjusted EPS guidance was raised to $30.40.
